source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A pinhole camera consists of a box in which an image is formed on the film plane which is a distance $P$ from a pinhole of diameter $d$. The object is at a distance $L$ from the pinhole, and light of wavelength $\nabla$ is used (Fig. 2.66). Approximately what diameter $d$ of the pinhole will give the best image resolution?

candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: A pinhole camera consists of a box in which an image is formed on the film plane which is a distance $P$ from a pinhole of diameter $d$. The object is at a distance $L$ from the pinhole, and light of wavelength $\nabla$ is used (Fig. 2.66). Approximately what diameter $d$ of the pinhole will give the best image resolution?
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL image object string representation rather than a file path or URL
answer field formula differs from the formula stated in the reasoning text
metadata.reasoning_chain:
By geometrical optics, a point on the object would cast a bright disk on the film of diameter $\Delta_1$ given by
$$
\frac{\Delta_1}{L+P} = \frac{d}{L}.
$$
Due to diffraction by the pinhole, the point would form a bright Airy disk on the film of diameter
$$
\Delta_2 \approx \frac{2\lambda P}{d}.
$$
The resultant diameter of the image is then
$$
\Delta = \Delta_1 + \Delta_2 = \frac{(L+P)d}{L} + \frac{2\lambda P}{d}.
$$
Minimizing $\Delta$ with respect to $d$ yields the optimal pinhole diameter:
$$
d = \sqrt{\frac{2\lambda L P}{L+P}}.
$$

By geometrical optics, a point on the object would cast a bright disk on the film of diameter $\Delta_1$ given by
$$
\frac{\Delta_1}{L+P} = \frac{d}{L}.

Due to diffraction by the pinhole, the point would form a bright Airy disk on the film of diameter
$$
\Delta_2 \approx \frac{2\lambda P}{d}.
$$
The resultant diameter of the image is then
$$
\Delta = \Delta_1 + \Delta_2 = \frac{(L+P)d}{L} + \frac{2\lambda P}{d}.
$$
Minimizing $\Delta$ with respect to $d$ yields the optimal pinhole diameter:
$$
d = \sqrt{\frac{2\lambda L P}{L+P}}.
$$
